title: ecb / staff projections / euro area | Economic Commentary | ECB staff macroeconomic projections for the euro area, March...
summary: The ECB staff macroeconomic projections for March 2026 show the euro area economy remained resilient in 2025 but faces renewed uncertainty from the Middle East conflict, with energy price shocks expected to push HICP inflation to 3.1% in Q2 2026 before declining. The baseline projects GDP growth of 0.9% in 2026, 1.3% in 2027, and 1.4% in 2028, with headline HICP inflation of 2.6%, 2.0%, and 2.1% respectively.
